author.name: Bjørn Torleiv Wahl
author.title: UI/UX Specialist
author.biography:
I'm Bjørn Torleiv Wahl, a UI/UX specialist based in Norway with a deep passion for building digital products that truly work for people. My career has been shaped by a curiosity for how design, technology, and teamwork intersect to create meaningful experiences.

Over the years I've had the privilege of working across industries, from media and publishing to enterprise SaaS. I've led design system initiatives, facilitated cross-functional workshops, and shipped products used by thousands. I believe that great design isn't just about pixels — it's about understanding problems, asking the right questions, and collaborating closely with engineers and stakeholders.

As a certified Scrum Master, I bring a structured yet flexible approach to product development. I thrive in environments where design thinking meets agile delivery, and I'm always looking for ways to bridge the gap between strategy and execution.

When I'm not designing, you'll find me behind a camera exploring landscapes, hiking Norwegian trails, or tinkering with side projects that keep my creative skills sharp. I'm always open to new opportunities and conversations — feel free to reach out.

projects.title: NTB Design System
projects.description: Built a design system that brought together 15+ apps under one roof. It supports multiple brands and teams, making it easier for everyone to design and ship products without starting from scratch every time.

details.heading: My Role
details.body: I led the design and implementation, starting from an existing foundation and adapting it to what the products actually needed. Created and refined over 100 components, wrote the docs, and established patterns everyone could follow. Basically, I tried to make something practical that teams would actually want to use.

details.heading: The Challenge
details.body: The apps had grown independently, which meant inconsistent experiences and a lot of duplicated work. There was no shared structure, so maintaining quality was tough. On top of that, the system needed to handle multi-tenancy without becoming overly complex.

details.heading: Results
details.body: Teams went from building in isolation to working from a shared foundation. Design and development moved faster, collaboration improved, and everything felt more consistent. It also made scaling and future improvements a lot more predictable.

projects.title: NTB Image Portal
projects.description: A media marketplace where news agencies can sell and distribute photos and videos globally. Made it simple for buyers to find what they need and easy for agencies to manage their content.

details.heading: My Role
details.body: Designed the entire user experience and interface from scratch. That meant figuring out the structure, mapping out workflows, and deciding what everything should look like. I worked closely with product owners, developers, and stakeholders to turn complex requirements into something that actually makes sense to use.

details.heading: The Challenge
details.body: The platform had to work for multiple agencies and customers across the globe, handling massive amounts of images and complex licensing rules. Starting from zero meant every decision had to balance flexibility with being easy to use. And it needed to be fast and reliable for professional users who depend on it for their work.

details.heading: Results
details.body: The platform ended up being intuitive and scalable. Agencies got a reliable way to distribute their content, and buyers got a clear, efficient way to find and buy what they need. Building on a solid foundation also made it easier to maintain and expand later.

projects.title: SDL Media Manager
projects.description: Professional software for managing 400+ million images and videos. News agencies depend on it daily to move content fast and reliably, and it powers a bunch of other tools too.

details.heading: My Role
details.body: Focused on making a complex platform easier to use. Simplified workflows, worked with developers and stakeholders to solve real problems without breaking what already works. The goal was to make everyday tasks less friction-filled.

details.heading: The Challenge
details.body: This platform handles an insane amount of content and teams depend on it for their work. Speed and reliability are non-negotiable. At the same time, it's the foundation for other apps, so changes can't break those integrations or disrupt existing workflows.

details.heading: Results
details.body: Made the platform more efficient in daily use. Finding, managing, and distributing content became smoother. It's easier to maintain and extend now, and the foundation got stronger for the products that depend on it.

projects.title: NTB Mediebank
projects.description: A digital asset management platform built to be simple and intuitive. It's used by teams across different industries, with over 40,000 active users who rely on it daily.

details.heading: My Role
details.body: Improved the interface and usability while also designing new features as the product grew. Since it was relatively new, a lot of my time went into figuring out workflows, building features, and establishing a solid foundation. I worked closely with developers and product stakeholders to make sure solutions actually worked for people.

details.heading: The Challenge
details.body: The system needed to work for teams in different industries, with editorial being the main group. It had to be straightforward while still supporting complex workflows and huge volumes of content. The tricky part was balancing speed and clarity with long-term scalability while many core features were still being defined.

details.heading: Results
details.body: The product became more complete and reliable. New features made it more useful for everyday work, and a better interface made it easier to learn. It also gave the platform a stronger foundation, making future development faster and more predictable.

projects.title: AVA Eiendom
projects.description: A website refresh that gives this real estate agency a stronger, more modern presence. Shows off their properties and services in a way that actually feels current.

details.heading: My Role
details.body: Designed the website and made sure the refreshed brand translated into a modern digital experience. Figured out the structure, designed the key pages, and made sure the visual direction matched their identity. Worked with stakeholders to align everything with their goals.

details.heading: The Challenge
details.body: The old website didn't match the quality of what they do or the properties they work with. Information was scattered, navigation was confusing, and it all felt outdated. The task was to modernize it while making it actually easy for visitors to understand what they offer and take the next step.

details.heading: Results
details.body: They got a stronger, more professional digital presence. Information is clearer and easier to find. It presents the agency with more confidence and helps attract new clients.

projects.title: CP-foreningen
projects.description: A membership portal that helps a patient organization connect with and support its members. Made it clearer and more accessible.

details.heading: My Role
details.body: Redesigned the existing portal with a focus on making it easier and more accessible for members. Improved the layout, navigation, and visual clarity, working within the constraints of what was already there. The goal was a calmer, clearer experience that better supported members.

details.heading: The Challenge
details.body: Since it's used by a patient organization, accessibility was critical. Members have different tech comfort levels, and the old design was hard to navigate. The platform itself couldn't be rebuilt, so improvements had to come through better structure, clearer design, and smarter interface choices.

details.heading: Results
details.body: The redesign made the portal easier and more accessible. Information is easier to find and the whole experience feels calmer and more approachable. It became a more useful tool that actually strengthened the connection between the organization and its members.

projects.title: Danvik Folkehøgskole
projects.description: A website for a folk high school designed to attract students. It showcases classes, trips, and student life in a way that's inviting and helps people take the next step.

details.heading: My Role
details.body: Designed the entire website from scratch, focusing on how to present the school in a way that actually appeals to potential students. Figured out the structure, designed the key pages, and worked with content and visuals to show what student life is really like. The goal was to make it feel honest and welcoming.

details.heading: The Challenge
details.body: The site needed to speak directly to prospective students and help them understand what the school offers. There's a lot of information to share, but it can't feel overwhelming. It also needed to naturally guide people toward applying without being pushy.

details.heading: Results
details.body: The new site made it much easier to explore the school and get a real sense of the experience. Information is clearer and the structure guides visitors naturally toward applying. It gave the school a stronger digital presence and a better tool for attracting new students.

projects.title: Dipr
projects.description: An alternative to traditional local news. It's about deeper journalism, taking your time, and building a real community instead of just an audience. It's become known for pioneering this approach.

details.heading: My Role
details.body: Worked on both the brand and the platform design, shaping its identity and reading experience from the ground up. Defined the visual direction, designed key parts of the application, and made sure the interface supported the editorial vision. The focus was creating a calm, centered experience where content takes center stage.

details.heading: The Challenge
details.body: The goal was to challenge traditional news by slowing things down and focusing on quality over volume. The design needed to feel different from typical news sites—which are often cluttered and chasing the latest updates. It had to feel trustworthy, thoughtful, and worth spending time with.

details.heading: Results
details.body: The result was a distinctive platform that supports long-form journalism and encourages deeper engagement. The branding and interface communicate its identity clearly and make the experience feel intentional. It helped establish the platform as a credible and recognizable alternative in its field.

projects.title: Munitize
projects.description: A marketplace and payment solution for game servers. Makes it easy for servers to earn money from their players, and simple for players to buy stuff in-game without friction.

details.heading: My Role
details.body: Worked on the brand and the platform design, figuring out its visual identity and core user experience. Designed key flows for browsing and buying digital goods. Early on, I ran a two-week Google Design Sprint to explore ideas and validate them, then tested prototypes with students from a local game dev school to make sure decisions were grounded in real user feedback.

details.heading: The Challenge
details.body: The platform had to make it easy for server owners to sell content while keeping things intuitive for players. Payments and accounts can quickly become confusing, so clarity and trust were everything. It also needed a strong identity to stand out in a space often filled with fragmented, inconsistent tools.

details.heading: Results
details.body: The result was a clean, easy-to-use platform with a strong and consistent identity. Server owners could manage and sell digital goods more easily, and players could complete purchases without extra friction. Early testing helped catch issues and made sure the platform was built on what users actually needed.

projects.title: Everyday
projects.description: A digital design and development studio that delivers high-end work for some of Norway's biggest brands. This is their own branding and website.

details.heading: My Role
details.body: Designed their visual identity and website, giving the studio a clearer and more distinctive presence. Created a flexible visual system and designed pages that showcase their work in a confident way. The balance was between being professional and having personality—sharp without feeling generic.

details.heading: The Challenge
details.body: They do high-quality work for major brands, but their own brand didn't reflect that. The challenge was creating something credible and polished while still showing character. In a sea of agencies that all look and sound the same, it needed to stand out.

details.heading: Results
details.body: They got a more confident and recognizable brand, backed by a website that felt modern and expressive. It helped communicate their level of quality more clearly and made their work feel more compelling. The new identity gave them a stronger foundation to grow from.

projects.title: Sørenga
projects.description: A housing project in prime Oslo locations. This is the branding and website to help residents and potential buyers understand the project.

details.heading: My Role
details.body: Worked on the branding and designed the website, focusing on making information clear and organized. Figured out the visual direction, structured the content, and designed pages that made it easy for residents and buyers to understand the project. The goal was clarity, readability, and cutting out the unnecessary complexity.

details.heading: The Challenge
details.body: Housing projects come with a lot of practical information, and it's easy for it to become overwhelming. The existing materials were hard to navigate and people struggled to find what they needed. The challenge was to simplify things and present information in a way that actually makes sense.

details.heading: Results
details.body: Residents and buyers can now easily find the information they need. The site presents the project clearly and makes it easier to understand what's being offered. It also strengthened the project's brand presence and credibility.

heading: The Challenge
body: The studio delivered high-quality work for major brands, but their own brand didn’t reflect that level of craft. The challenge was to create something that felt credible and polished, while still showing character. It needed to stand out in a crowded space of agencies that all tend to look and sound the same.

heading: Results
body: The result was a more confident and recognizable brand, supported by a website that felt modern, expressive, and true to the studio. It helped communicate their level of quality more clearly and made their work feel more compelling. The new identity gave them a stronger foundation to present themselves and grow.
